agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (e.g., W, F, Y) will enhance complement

report.hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (e.g., W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (e.g., L, I, V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/5 binding by avoiding unfavorable electrostatic interactions. This will increase the selectivity margin over native SST-14.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 binding but not sufficient select

report.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 binding but not sufficient selectivity. The next iteration should focus on disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to improve Δmargin and achieve true SSTR2 selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions where off-target contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 are likely to occur. Mutations at these positions can disrupt conserved pocket interactions while preserving the pharmacophore.

Introduce mutations at key non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. These mutations are predicted to reduce off-target binding while maintaining the core pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 + Cys3/Cys14).
